verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70-1) is a canonical cytosolic/nuclear chaperone with no signal peptide or TM domain. However, it is well-documented to appear on the plasma membrane of stressed and tumor cells (membrane Hsp70), where it serves as a tumor-selective target for the antibody cmHsp70.1 and the TKD-peptide-based NK cell therapy (e.g., Multimmune's clinical programs). Surface display is induced by stress/transformation, not baseline in normal cells. Not GPI-anchored; surface association involves lipid (phosphatidylserine/Gb3) interactions on tumor PM. This fits cell_state_induced contextual surface exposure rather than constitutive accessibility.
